_schema_notes: Each technique has TWO severity assessments: (1) top-level 'severity' is editorially assigned tactical severity (how bad if it succeeds), (2) 'niss.severity' is computed from the NISS vector measuring biological/neural signal disruption. These may differ — a man-in-the-middle attack is tactically critical but has low biological impact (NISS 2.7). When presenting both, clarify which is which.

techniques.name: Signal injection
techniques.mechanism: Electrical current delivery at electrode-tissue interface modulating local field potentials
sources: Kohno et al. 2009
Bonaci et al. 2015
techniques.therapeutic_analog: tDCS/tACS neuromodulation

techniques.name: Neural ransomware
techniques.mechanism: Disruption or conditional locking of neural function via closed-loop stimulation parameter manipulation
sources: Pycroft et al. 2016
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) / Responsive neurostimulation (RNS)

techniques.name: Eavesdropping / signal interception
techniques.mechanism: Passive capture of neural electromagnetic emissions from BCI data pathways
sources: Kohno et al. 2009
Landau et al. 2020
Schroder et al. 2025
techniques.therapeutic_analog: EEG/ECoG diagnostic monitoring

techniques.name: Man-in-the-middle
techniques.mechanism: Active interception and modification of signals between BCI components in transit
sources: Martinovic et al. 2012
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Signal routing in closed-loop neuroprosthetics

techniques.name: Quantum tunneling exploit
techniques.mechanism: Exploitation of quantum tunneling effects at nanoscale electrode-tissue junctions
sources: Summhammer et al. 2012
Salari et al. 2015
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Quantum sensing for neural diagnostics (NV-center magnetometry)

techniques.name: Davydov soliton attack
techniques.mechanism: Davydov soliton propagation in protein alpha-helices at electrode-tissue interface
sources: Davydov 1973
Scott 1992
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Biophoton/soliton-based cellular signaling research

techniques.name: Protocol manipulation
techniques.mechanism: Manipulation of BCI communication protocol handshakes, headers, or sequencing
sources: Schroder et al. 2025
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Adaptive stimulation protocol adjustment in closed-loop BCIs

techniques.name: Command hijacking
techniques.mechanism: Interception and substitution of BCI motor/sensory command signals
sources: Kohno et al. 2009
techniques.therapeutic_analog: BCI command interfaces for motor-impaired patients

techniques.name: RF false brainwave injection
techniques.mechanism: Radiofrequency emission at neural oscillation frequencies to induce false brain activity patterns
sources: Zhang et al. 2024
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS)

techniques.name: ELF neural entrainment
techniques.mechanism: Extremely low frequency electromagnetic fields entraining endogenous neural oscillations via resonance
sources: NTIA Frequency Allocation Chart
US Navy ELF program (declassified)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Audio-visual entrainment (AVE) / photic driving therapy

techniques.name: Intermodulation (BCI signal weaponized)
techniques.mechanism: Nonlinear mixing of two or more carrier frequencies in neural tissue producing intermodulation products at neural frequencies
sources: ITU Radio Regulations
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Temporal interference (TI) deep brain stimulation

techniques.name: Pulsed microwave (Frey effect)
techniques.mechanism: Pulsed microwave radiation inducing thermoelastic expansion in neural tissue (Frey effect / microwave auditory effect)
sources: Frey 1962
Lin 1978
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Transcranial microwave stimulation (experimental)

techniques.name: Temporal interference (deep targeting)
techniques.mechanism: Two high-frequency currents intersecting in deep brain tissue, with stimulation occurring only at the interference zone
sources: Grossman et al. 2017 (Cell)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Temporal interference (TI) non-invasive deep brain stimulation

techniques.name: Envelope modulation (stealth carrier)
techniques.mechanism: Amplitude-modulated carrier signal where neural tissue demodulates the envelope at biologically active frequencies
sources: Datta et al. 2009
techniques.therapeutic_analog: tACS with carrier-envelope paradigm

techniques.name: Directed energy (thermal I0 damage)
techniques.mechanism: Focused electromagnetic energy causing thermal damage to electrode-tissue interface or neural tissue
sources: US DoD Active Denial System (declassified)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Thermal ablation (stereotactic radiosurgery, LITT, RF ablation)

techniques.name: Professor X backdoor (training-time)
techniques.mechanism: Backdoor insertion during BCI model training phase via poisoned training data or modified training process
sources: Zhang et al. 2024 (Professor X attack on EEG models)

techniques.name: Transfer learning backdoor
techniques.mechanism: Backdoor propagation via transfer learning from compromised pre-trained BCI model
sources: Meng et al. 2024

techniques.name: Adversarial filter attack
techniques.mechanism: Crafted input perturbations that pass through BCI signal filters to reach decoder/classifier
sources: Liu et al. 2024

techniques.name: Universal adversarial perturbation (UAP)
techniques.mechanism: Single perturbation vector effective against any input sample to a BCI classifier
sources: Liu et al. 2024
Moosavi-Dezfooli et al. 2017

techniques.name: Membership inference on neural data
techniques.mechanism: Statistical inference to determine if specific neural data was used in BCI model training
sources: Shokri et al. 2017
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Clinical trial participation verification

techniques.name: Federated gradient leakage
techniques.mechanism: Reconstruction of individual neural data from shared gradient updates in federated BCI training
sources: Zhu et al. 2019
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Federated learning for multi-site clinical BCI trials

techniques.name: Neurofeedback falsification
techniques.mechanism: Falsification of neurofeedback display or stimulation in closed-loop therapeutic BCIs
sources: Meng et al. 2024
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Neurofeedback therapy

techniques.name: Closed-loop perturbation cascade
techniques.mechanism: Cascading perturbation in closed-loop stimulation systems where output feeds back as input
sources: Meng et al. 2024
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Closed-loop responsive neurostimulation (RNS)

techniques.name: Training data poisoning (model bias injection)
techniques.mechanism: Systematic bias injection into BCI training datasets to skew model behavior
sources: Biggio et al. 2012; Goldblum et al. 2022

techniques.name: Neuronal jamming
techniques.mechanism: Broadband electromagnetic interference overwhelming neural signal acquisition
sources: Lopez-Moreno et al. 2024 (Murcia taxonomy)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: TMS (cortical suppression / virtual lesion technique)

techniques.name: Neuronal flooding
techniques.mechanism: Excessive stimulation current overwhelming normal neural firing patterns
sources: Lopez-Moreno et al. 2024 (Murcia taxonomy)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Electroconvulsive therapy (ECT)

techniques.name: Neuronal scanning
techniques.mechanism: Systematic probing of neural response patterns to map BCI topology and individual neural architecture
sources: Lopez-Moreno et al. 2024 (Murcia taxonomy)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Brain mapping for surgical planning / functional localization

techniques.name: Neural selective forwarding
techniques.mechanism: Selective forwarding or dropping of specific neural signal types in a multi-channel BCI pipeline
sources: Lopez-Moreno et al. 2024 (Murcia taxonomy)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Selective neural signal routing in closed-loop therapy

techniques.name: Neural DoS (stimulation flood)
techniques.mechanism: Sustained high-rate stimulation exceeding neural tissue recovery capacity
sources: Kohno et al. 2009
techniques.therapeutic_analog: High-frequency stimulation for neural suppression (DBS at >100 Hz)

techniques.name: Motor hijacking
techniques.mechanism: Hijacking motor cortex output signals to produce involuntary movement via BCI motor interface
sources: Kohno et al. 2009
Schroder et al. 2025
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Functional electrical stimulation (FES) / brain-controlled prosthetics

techniques.name: Battery drain attack (resource depletion)
techniques.mechanism: Accelerated battery depletion of implanted BCI through sustained high-power operation
sources: Martin et al. 2004 (implant battery attacks)

techniques.name: Identity spoofing (neural biometric)
techniques.mechanism: Replication or synthesis of individual neural biometric signatures to impersonate BCI users
sources: Maiorana et al. 2016
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Neural biometric authentication for medical device access

techniques.name: Identity erosion (long-term personality drift)
techniques.mechanism: Gradual modification of personality-linked neural patterns through sustained BCI-mediated influence
sources: Yuste et al. 2017 (Neurorights)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Neuroplasticity-based rehabilitation / personality change monitoring in DBS

techniques.name: Working memory poisoning
techniques.mechanism: Targeted disruption of working memory maintenance via interference with sustained neural oscillations
sources: Bonaci et al. 2015
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Working memory training / tDCS-enhanced cognitive training

techniques.name: P300 interrogation
techniques.mechanism: Extraction of private information via P300 event-related potential responses to probe stimuli
sources: Bonaci et al. 2015
Martinovic et al. 2012
techniques.therapeutic_analog: P300 BCI (communication for locked-in patients)

techniques.name: Thought decoding (covert speech)
techniques.mechanism: Decoding internal speech or intended communication from neural activity patterns
sources: Willett et al. 2023 (Nature)
Meta FAIR 2023
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Speech neuroprosthetics (neural speech decoding)

techniques.name: Agency manipulation
techniques.mechanism: Manipulation of sense of agency (ownership of actions/thoughts) via BCI-mediated stimulation or feedback
sources: Yuste et al. 2017 (Neurorights)
Goering et al. 2021
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Neurofeedback for self-regulation / agency restoration post-stroke

techniques.name: Brainprint theft
techniques.mechanism: Extraction and replication of unique neural identity signatures (brainprint) from BCI data
sources: Maiorana et al. 2016
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Brain fingerprinting / neural identity verification

techniques.name: Self-model corruption
techniques.mechanism: Disruption of neural self-model (body ownership, self-awareness, narrative identity) via sustained BCI manipulation
sources: Yuste et al. 2017 (Neurorights)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Psychedelic-assisted therapy (controlled self-model disruption); rubber hand illusion research

techniques.name: Neurophishing
techniques.mechanism: Social engineering via BCI-mediated trust manipulation or subliminal stimuli
sources: Frank et al. 2024
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Subliminal priming research / implicit cognitive assessment

techniques.name: Cognitive biometric inference
techniques.mechanism: Inference of cognitive traits, emotional states, or health conditions from BCI signal patterns
sources: Landau et al. 2020
techniques.therapeutic_analog: EEG-based cognitive and psychiatric assessment

techniques.name: BLE/RF side-channel
techniques.mechanism: Side-channel information leakage from BCI wireless communications (BLE, WiFi, proprietary RF)
sources: Bonaci et al. 2015
Schroder et al. 2025

techniques.name: Supply chain compromise (firmware backdoor)
techniques.mechanism: Malicious modification of BCI hardware or firmware during manufacturing, distribution, or maintenance
sources: Schroder et al. 2025

techniques.name: Cloud infrastructure attack
techniques.mechanism: Compromise of cloud infrastructure processing BCI data (storage, compute, APIs)
sources: Schroder et al. 2025

techniques.name: Harvest-now-decrypt-later
techniques.mechanism: Collection and storage of encrypted neural data for future decryption when quantum computers become available
sources: Gidney 2025 (Google quantum roadmap)

techniques.name: OTA firmware weaponization
techniques.mechanism: Weaponization of over-the-air firmware update mechanism to deliver malicious BCI firmware
sources: Schroder et al. 2025

techniques.name: Mass BCI compromise (platform attack)
techniques.mechanism: Simultaneous compromise of many BCI devices via shared platform vulnerability
sources: Schroder et al. 2025 (Yale worst-case scenario)

techniques.name: Electrode compromise (physical tamper)
techniques.mechanism: Physical tampering with implanted or wearable BCI electrodes
sources: Kohno et al. 2009

techniques.name: Wireless authentication bypass
techniques.mechanism: Exploitation of weak or absent authentication on BCI wireless interfaces
sources: Schroder et al. 2025 (Yale)

techniques.name: Hardware fault injection (voltage/EM glitching)
techniques.mechanism: Inducing hardware faults via voltage glitching, EM pulse, or laser injection to bypass BCI security
sources: Bar-El et al. 2006 (fault injection survey)

techniques.name: Neural data privacy breach
techniques.mechanism: Unauthorized access to or exfiltration of recorded neural data
sources: Landau et al. 2020
GDPR Article 9
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Clinical neural data management for treatment optimization

techniques.name: ERP harvesting
techniques.mechanism: Harvesting event-related potentials (ERPs) to extract cognitive responses to specific stimuli
sources: Bonaci et al. 2015
Martinovic et al. 2012
techniques.therapeutic_analog: ERP-based clinical diagnostics

techniques.name: Cognitive state capture
techniques.mechanism: Real-time capture and classification of cognitive states (attention, emotion, fatigue, deception) from BCI signals
sources: Landau et al. 2020
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Brain-state dependent therapy timing

techniques.name: Memory extraction
techniques.mechanism: Extraction of memory-related neural patterns (episodic, semantic, or procedural) from BCI recordings
sources: Bonaci et al. 2015
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Memory retrieval and consolidation enhancement therapy

techniques.name: BCI cognitive warfare
techniques.mechanism: Coordinated manipulation of cognitive function across populations via compromised BCI infrastructure
sources: NATO STO 2024 (Cognitive Warfare concept)
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Population-scale cognitive manipulation is categorically prohibited

techniques.name: Neuro-surveillance
techniques.mechanism: Continuous covert monitoring of neural activity for surveillance purposes
sources: Ienca & Andorno 2017 (Neurorights)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Continuous EEG monitoring (epilepsy, ICU)

techniques.name: Network mapping (BCI topology)
techniques.mechanism: Mapping BCI network topology, device capabilities, and communication pathways
sources: Schroder et al. 2025
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Brain network analysis for diagnosis and surgical planning

techniques.name: Calibration poisoning
techniques.mechanism: Poisoning BCI calibration process to establish persistent attacker advantage
sources: Schroder et al. 2025
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Adaptive BCI calibration for patients with changing neural dynamics

techniques.name: Pattern lock (learned pathway persistence)
techniques.mechanism: Exploitation of learned neural pathway persistence to maintain BCI-mediated influence across sessions
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Motor learning and neural rehabilitation (learned pathway strengthening)

techniques.name: Memory implant (cross-session persistence)
techniques.mechanism: Implanting persistent information into neural memory systems via BCI-mediated stimulation during consolidation
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Memory consolidation enhancement (targeted memory reactivation during sleep)

techniques.name: Coherence mimicry
techniques.mechanism: Generating signals that pass QIF coherence metric validation while carrying malicious payload
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Coherence-based neurofeedback (SMR/beta coherence training)

techniques.name: Gradual drift (slow parameter shift)
techniques.mechanism: Slow, sub-threshold modification of BCI parameters to avoid threshold-based detection
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Gradual dose titration in neurostimulation therapy

techniques.name: Noise injection (detection masking)
techniques.mechanism: Injection of noise into BCI data pipeline to mask ongoing attack signatures
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Stochastic resonance (SR) — adding noise to enhance weak signal detection

techniques.name: User consent fatigue (neural permission flooding)
techniques.mechanism: Flooding BCI user with permission requests until cognitive fatigue leads to reflexive approval
sources: Felt et al. 2012 (permission fatigue); Bonaci et al. 2015
techniques.therapeutic_analog: UX design for cognitive accessibility in medical BCIs

techniques.name: Algorithmic psychosis induction (recommendation weaponization)
techniques.mechanism: Weaponization of algorithmic recommendation systems to induce psychotic-like cognitive states via BCI-amplified content
sources: Haidt & Twenge 2023 (social media & mental health); Bail et al. 2018 (echo chambers & polarization); Hao 2021 (Facebook internal research, WSJ); Gonzalez-Bailon et al. 2023 (algorithmic feed effects); Howes & Kapur 2009 (dopamine hypothesis of schizophrenia); Kelleher et al. 2012 (psychotic-like experiences & environmental risk factors); Montague et al. 2012 (computational psychiatry)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Therapeutic content recommendation for mental health

techniques.name: Slow drift / boiling frog (adiabatic phase space manipulation)
techniques.mechanism: Adiabatic parameter manipulation along neural phase space paths that avoid detection thresholds
sources: Izhikevich 2007 (Dynamical Systems in Neuroscience); Strogatz 2015 (Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos); Breakspear 2017 (Dynamic models of large-scale brain activity, Nature Neuroscience)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Slow DBS parameter optimization (adiabatic adjustment protocols)

techniques.name: Phase dynamics replay/mimicry (GAN-synthesized or RF-injected neural trajectories)
techniques.mechanism: Replay or synthesis of neural signal trajectories that reproduce legitimate dynamical system behavior
sources: Martinovic et al. 2012 (On the Feasibility of Side-Channel Attacks with BCIs, USENIX Security); Marcel & Millan 2007 (Person authentication using brainwaves); Zhang et al. 2024 (ATGAN attention-based temporal GAN for EEG); Hartmann et al. 2025 (EEG-GAN toolkit); Fosch-Villaronga et al. 2023 (Brain-Hack: RF injection across BCI types); Frank et al. 2017 (Subliminal probing, NDSS); Chen et al. 2024 (Professor X backdoor); Bonaci et al. 2014 (Neurofeedback hijacking)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Sensory prosthetics (cochlear implants, retinal prostheses, somatosensory feedback)

techniques.name: Bifurcation forcing (critical parameter manipulation to trigger neural state transitions)
techniques.mechanism: Manipulation of neural parameters toward bifurcation points to trigger catastrophic state transitions
sources: Izhikevich 2007 (Dynamical Systems in Neuroscience — bifurcation classification); Breakspear 2017 (Dynamic models of large-scale brain activity); Scheffer et al. 2009 (Early-warning signals for critical transitions, Nature); Maturana et al. 2020 (Critical slowing down as biomarker for seizure); Jirsa et al. 2014 (On the nature of seizure dynamics, Brain)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Responsive neurostimulation at seizure bifurcation (RNS/NeuroPace)

techniques.name: Separatrix leakage (meta-biometric extraction from dynamical transition observations)
techniques.mechanism: Extraction of individual neural identity from dynamical system transition observations (separatrix geometry fingerprinting)
sources: Finn et al. 2015 (Functional connectome fingerprinting, Nature Neuroscience); Martinovic et al. 2012 (P300 side-channel, USENIX Security); Da Silva Castanheira et al. 2021 (Individual brain charting, NeuroImage); Izhikevich 2007 (separatrix geometry in neural models)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Brain state detection for seizure prediction (transition pattern monitoring)

techniques.name: Integrator/resonator type switching (tonic excitability manipulation)
techniques.mechanism: Switching neurons between integrator and resonator computational modes via sustained tonic current injection
sources: Izhikevich 2007 (Dynamical Systems in Neuroscience — integrator/resonator classification); Prescott et al. 2008 (Biophysical basis for three distinct dynamical mechanisms of action potential initiation, PLoS Computational Biology)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Excitability modulation in epilepsy and pain management

techniques.name: Baseline adaptation poisoning (re-enrollment window exploitation)
techniques.mechanism: Exploitation of BCI re-enrollment windows to inject poisoned baseline neural data
sources: Bigdely-Shamlo et al. 2015 (Brain activity-based image classification, Journal of Neural Engineering); Arias-Cabarcos et al. 2021 (EEG biometric system attacks); Debie et al. 2020 (Cross-session/cross-device replay)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Adaptive baseline recalibration for changing patient conditions

techniques.name: Transducer inversion (acoustic eavesdropping via speaker-to-microphone reprogramming)
techniques.mechanism: Electromagnetic transducer bidirectionality exploited via codec register retasking to convert audio output hardware into covert microphone
sources: Guri et al. 2017 (SPEAKE(a)R: Turn Speakers to Microphones for Fun and Profit, arXiv:1611.07350)
RealTek HD Audio Codec specification (jack retasking registers)
Deshotels 2014 (Inaudible sound as a covert channel)

techniques.name: Ear-canal neural eavesdropping via modified consumer earbud (supply chain in-ear EEG)
techniques.mechanism: Conductive ear tip and embedded biopotential amplifier in consumer earbud captures in-ear EEG from temporal cortex via ear canal proximity
sources: Kaveh et al. 2020 (In-ear EEG: robust, unobtrusive, automatic, IEEE Trans Biomed Eng)
Idun Technologies 2023 (Guardian in-ear EEG sensor)
Debener et al. 2015 (cEEGrid: compact ear-EEG, J Neural Eng)
Bleichner & Debener 2017 (Concealed, unobtrusive ear-centered EEG acquisition, Front Hum Neurosci)
Looney et al. 2012 (In-the-ear recording concept, IEEE EMBC)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: In-ear EEG for seizure detection, sleep staging, cognitive monitoring

techniques.name: Cognitive inference from longitudinal in-ear EEG (personalized cognitive profiling)
techniques.mechanism: Machine learning model trained on longitudinal in-ear EEG data to infer cognitive states, build personalized profiles, and enable adaptive neural-targeted content manipulation
sources: Martinovic et al. 2012 (On the Feasibility of Side-Channel Attacks with Brain-Computer Interfaces, USENIX Security)
Landau et al. 2020 (Mind Reading: An Idea Whose Time Has Come?)
Roy et al. 2019 (Deep learning-based EEG analysis, J Neural Eng)
Kostas et al. 2021 (BENDR: EEG foundation model, Front Hum Neurosci)
Chen et al. 2024 (Large-scale EEG pre-training for cognitive state decoding)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Longitudinal EEG monitoring for neurofeedback and cognitive rehabilitation

techniques.name: Ultrasonic sonar vital sign extraction (inaudible Doppler physiological sensing)
techniques.mechanism: Inaudible ultrasonic continuous-wave emission from consumer speaker with Doppler shift analysis of reflected signal to extract cardiac and respiratory micro-movements
sources: Wang et al. 2019 (UltraSense: contactless vital sign monitoring via ultrasound, ACM MobiSys)
Nandakumar et al. 2015 (Contactless Sleep Apnea Detection on Smartphones, ACM MobiSys)
Xu et al. 2019 (Waveear: phone-based ear biometric via ultrasonic, IEEE INFOCOM)
Ling et al. 2020 (UltraGesture: fine-grained gesture recognition via ultrasonic)
Google Pixel Soli/Nest Hub Sleep Sensing (production deployment of radar/ultrasonic vital signs)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Contactless vital sign monitoring for sleep studies and elder care

techniques.name: Haptic motor body sonar (taptic engine repurposing for tissue impedance profiling)
techniques.mechanism: Haptic actuator driven at swept frequencies against body surface; accelerometer measures tissue acoustic impedance response for composition profiling
sources: Ding et al. 2020 (Body-area acoustic sensing: skin vibration for health monitoring)
Chen et al. 2021 (EarHealth: in-ear acoustic sensing for health monitoring, ACM IMWUT)
Yao et al. 2022 (Vibration-based tissue characterization with consumer wearables)
Apple Taptic Engine patents (linear resonant actuator, broadband excitation capability)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Acoustic impedance spectroscopy for body composition and edema monitoring

techniques.name: IR vascular mapping via Face ID system (NIR hemoglobin absorption imaging)
techniques.mechanism: Face ID IR dot projector (940nm) captures subsurface vascular topology via differential hemoglobin absorption; raw IR data extracted from compromised Secure Enclave pipeline
sources: Apple TrueDepth Camera System (Face ID, 30,000 IR dots at 940nm)
Crouzet et al. 2016 (Cerebrovascular mapping using NIR, NeuroImage)
Gupta et al. 2020 (Vein pattern recognition biometrics, IEEE Access)
Krishnaswamy & Baranoski 2004 (Bio-physically based rendering of human skin)
Hardeberg & Farup 2004 (NIR skin penetration depth models)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Near-infrared spectroscopy (NIRS) for cerebral and peripheral vascular imaging

techniques.name: LiDAR remote pulse detection (laser Doppler vibrometry for cardiac waveform extraction)
techniques.mechanism: iPhone VCSEL LiDAR array measures skin surface micro-vibrations via Doppler shift in reflected 940nm laser; pulse waveform extracted from raw SPAD photodetector timing data
sources: Apple iPhone LiDAR Scanner (dToF, 940nm VCSEL array, introduced iPhone 12 Pro)
Bernal et al. 2018 (Non-contact physiological monitoring using laser Doppler vibrometry)
Antognoli et al. 2020 (Contactless heart rate measurement via laser Doppler vibrometry, Sensors)
Sun et al. 2022 (Smartphone LiDAR for physiological sensing, IEEE Sensors J)
Rothberg et al. 2017 (Comprehensive review of laser Doppler vibrometry, Opt Lasers Eng)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Laser Doppler vibrometry for contactless cardiac monitoring

techniques.name: Ear canal acoustic fingerprinting (ANC echo profiling for covert identification)
techniques.mechanism: ANC earbud speaker emits probe tone; feedback microphone captures ear canal echo profile shaped by unique anatomical geometry; acoustic transfer function serves as biometric identifier
sources: NEC Corporation 2016 (Ear acoustic authentication technology)
Gao et al. 2019 (EarEcho: Continuous ear canal authentication, ACM IMWUT)
Fan et al. 2021 (HeadFi: continuous earphone authentication using ear biometrics, ACM SenSys)
Apple AirPods Pro ANC system (feedforward + feedback microphones, adaptive transparency)
Akkermans et al. 2005 (Acoustic ear recognition, IEEE ICB)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Ear canal acoustic authentication for medical device access control

techniques.name: Gyroscope acoustic eavesdropping (MEMS speech capture via resonant frequency aliasing)
techniques.mechanism: MEMS gyroscope mechanical resonance captures airborne acoustic vibrations; speech features reconstructed via signal processing of motion sensor output
sources: Michalevsky et al. 2014 (Gyrophone: Recognizing Speech from Gyroscope Signals, USENIX Security)
Zhang et al. 2017 (AccelWord: Energy-efficient voice command recognition via accelerometer)
Anand & Saxena 2018 (Speechless: Analyzing the Threat to Speech Privacy from Smartphone Motion Sensors, IEEE S&P)

techniques.name: Accelerometer speech reconstruction (vibration-to-audio via surface-coupled MEMS)
techniques.mechanism: MEMS accelerometer captures speech vibrations transmitted through surfaces or phone chassis; deep learning reconstructs intelligible audio from motion sensor data
sources: Zhang et al. 2019 (AccelEve: Eavesdropping via Accelerometers on Smartphones, IEEE S&P Workshop)
Ba et al. 2020 (Learning-based Practical Smartphone Eavesdropping with Built-in Accelerometer, NDSS)
Han et al. 2012 (ACComplice: accelerometer side channel, ACM CCS)

techniques.name: Ultrasonic cross-device tracking (inaudible beacon correlation for user identification)
techniques.mechanism: Inaudible ultrasonic beacons (18-22 kHz) embedded in audio content detected by consumer device microphones for cross-device user tracking and location correlation
sources: Mavroudis et al. 2017 (On the Privacy and Security of the Ultrasound Ecosystem, PoPETS)
Arp et al. 2017 (Privacy Threats through Ultrasonic Side Channels on Mobile Devices, IEEE EuroS&P)
Silverpush, Lisnr, SilverPush SDK (commercial ultrasonic tracking)

techniques.name: Acoustic keystroke inference (typing sound classification for credential extraction)
techniques.mechanism: Deep learning classification of keyboard acoustic emanations to reconstruct typed text including credentials and sensitive content
sources: Harrison & Matyunin 2023 (A Practical Deep Learning-Based Acoustic Side Channel Attack on Keyboards, IEEE European Symposium on Security and Privacy Workshops)
Zhuang et al. 2009 (Keyboard Acoustic Emanations Revisited, ACM TISSEC)
Compagno et al. 2017 (Don't Skype & Type! Acoustic Eavesdropping in Voice-Over-IP, ACM CCS)

techniques.name: Remote photoplethysmography (camera-based pulse and blood oxygen extraction)
techniques.mechanism: RGB camera captures sub-pixel skin color variations from cardiac blood volume pulses; deep learning extracts heart rate, HRV, respiratory rate, and SpO2 from video
sources: Verkruysse et al. 2008 (Remote plethysmographic imaging using ambient light, Optics Express)
Poh et al. 2011 (Advancements in Noncontact, Multiparameter Physiological Measurements Using a Webcam, IEEE Trans Biomed Eng)
Chen & McDuff 2018 (DeepPhys: Video-Based Physiological Measurement Using Convolutional Attention Networks, ECCV)
Liu et al. 2023 (EfficientPhys: enabling simple, fast and accurate camera-based vitals measurement)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Contactless vital sign monitoring for telemedicine and patient screening

techniques.name: Eye tracking cognitive state inference (gaze pattern analysis for attention and intent profiling)
techniques.mechanism: Eye tracking hardware in AR/VR headsets captures gaze patterns, pupil dilation, saccades, and fixations; ML models infer cognitive states, personality traits, and intent
sources: Katsini et al. 2020 (The Role of Eye Gaze in Security and Privacy Applications, ACM Computing Surveys)
Sluganovic et al. 2018 (Using Reflexive Eye Movements for Fast Challenge-Response Authentication, USENIX Security)
Apple Vision Pro eye tracking (visionOS gaze-based interaction)
Meta Quest Pro eye tracking (Meta Presence Platform)
Liebling & Preibusch 2014 (Privacy of Web Search via Eye Tracking, PoPETS)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Eye tracking for neurological assessment and cognitive rehabilitation

techniques.name: Ambient light sensor side-channel exfiltration (screen content inference via reflected light)
techniques.mechanism: Ambient light sensor captures display-reflected light variations to infer screen content, scrolling patterns, and user activity without requiring camera or screen capture permissions
sources: Zhang & Lian 2024 (Eavesdropping on Controller Area Network via Ambient Light Sensor, ACM CCS)
Spreitzer et al. 2018 (Systematic Classification of Side-Channel Attacks: A Case Study for Mobile Devices, IEEE Communications Surveys)
Mosenia et al. 2017 (PinMe: Tracking a Smartphone User around the World, IEEE Trans Multi-Scale Computing Systems)
mitigations: Safety ceiling: ALS sampling rate should be limited when screen content could be inferred; permission model should gate high-frequency ALS access

techniques.name: Accelerometer keystroke inference (touchscreen tap localization for PIN/password recovery)
techniques.mechanism: Accelerometer and gyroscope capture micro-tilt patterns from touchscreen taps; ML models localize tap positions to recover PINs, passwords, and typed text
sources: Owusu et al. 2012 (ACCessory: Password Inference using Accelerometers on Smartphones, ACM HotMobile)
Cai & Chen 2011 (TouchLogger: Inferring Keystrokes on Touch Screen from Smartphone Motion, USENIX HotSec)
Miluzzo et al. 2012 (TapPrints: Your Finger Taps Have Fingerprints, ACM MobiSys)

techniques.name: Gait biometric identification (IMU-based walking pattern fingerprint for persistent tracking)
techniques.mechanism: Smartphone IMU sensors capture unique walking patterns determined by biomechanics and neurological motor control; ML models fingerprint individuals for persistent tracking
sources: Muaaz & Mayrhofer 2017 (Smartphone-based Gait Recognition: From Authentication to Imitation, IEEE Trans Mobile Computing)
Ngo et al. 2014 (The Largest Inertial Sensor-Based Gait Database, Pattern Recognition)
Sprager & Juric 2015 (Inertial Sensor-Based Gait Recognition: A Review, Sensors)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Gait analysis for rehabilitation and neurological monitoring

techniques.name: Tremor and movement neurological profiling (IMU-based motor disorder detection and health inference)
techniques.mechanism: Smartphone IMU sensors detect pathological tremor frequencies and fine motor control degradation to infer neurological conditions, medication effects, and cognitive state
sources: Arora et al. 2015 (Detecting and monitoring the symptoms of Parkinson's disease using smartphones, J Med Internet Res)
Bot et al. 2016 (mPower: a smartphone study of Parkinson disease, Scientific Data)
Giancardo et al. 2016 (Computer keyboard interaction as an indicator of early Parkinson's disease, Scientific Reports)
Adams et al. 2017 (Tremor detection from smartphone accelerometer data, IEEE EMBC)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Remote Parkinson's monitoring and neurological screening

techniques.name: WiFi CSI passive body sensing (through-wall vital signs, 3D pose reconstruction, respiratory and gait biometric inference via dedicated or commodity WiFi hardware)
techniques.mechanism: WiFi OFDM subcarrier amplitude and phase modulation by human body movement, respiration, and gait; works through walls without any device on the target; both dedicated CSI hardware and commodity WiFi
sources: Liu et al. 2019 (Wireless Sensing for Human Activity: A Survey, IEEE Communications Surveys)
Liu et al. 2015 (WiFi respiration monitoring)
Zeng et al. 2020 (FarSense: pushing the range limit of WiFi-based respiration sensing, ACM MobiSys)
Wang et al. 2017 (TensorBeat: Tensor Decomposition for Monitoring Multiperson Breathing Beats with Commodity WiFi, ACM Trans Intelligent Systems and Technology)
Wang et al. 2016 (WiFi-based gait recognition)
Jiang et al. 2020 (Towards 3D Human Pose Construction Using WiFi, ACM MobiCom)
Ali et al. 2015 (WiKey: keystroke recognition from WiFi CSI, MobiCom)
Ma et al. 2019 (WiFi sensing with CSI: a survey, ACM Computing Surveys)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Contactless vital sign monitoring for sleep studies, elder care, and post-surgical recovery

techniques.name: BLE physical-layer device fingerprinting (radio frequency imperfection tracking)
techniques.mechanism: Passive BLE receiver extracts unique physical-layer radio imperfections (CFO, I/Q imbalance) from BLE advertisements to track devices despite MAC address randomization
sources: Becker et al. 2022 (Tracking Anonymized Bluetooth Devices, PoPETS)
Das et al. 2018 (Tracking Mobile Web Users Through Motion Sensors, NDSS)
Ramsey et al. 2020 (BLE Device Tracking via Physical Layer Fingerprinting, IEEE CNS)

techniques.name: Thermal facial stress and emotion inference (IR thermography for autonomic nervous system state extraction)
techniques.mechanism: Thermal IR camera captures facial temperature distribution modulated by autonomic nervous system; ML models infer stress, cognitive load, emotion, and deception from thermal patterns
sources: Engert et al. 2014 (Exploring the Use of Thermal Imaging to Assess Stress, PLOS ONE)
Abdelrahman et al. 2017 (Cognitive Heat: Exploring the Usage of Thermal Imaging to Unobtrusively Estimate Cognitive Load, ACM IMWUT)
Pavlidis et al. 2002 (Thermal Image Analysis for Polygraph Testing, IEEE Engineering in Medicine and Biology)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Thermal imaging for pain assessment and autonomic function testing

techniques.name: PPG pulse waveform biometric identification (cardiac signature fingerprinting via wearable optical sensor)
techniques.mechanism: Wearable PPG sensor captures unique cardiac pulse waveform morphology determined by cardiovascular physiology; deep learning extracts biometric identity from waveform features
sources: Biswas et al. 2019 (CorNET: Deep Learning Framework for PPG-Based Heart Rate Estimation and Biometric Identification, IEEE Trans Biomed Eng)
Yadav et al. 2018 (Evaluation of PPG biometrics for authentication, IEEE ICB)
Kavsaoglu et al. 2023 (PPG-based biometric identification: A comprehensive review, Expert Systems with Applications)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: PPG-based cardiovascular health monitoring

techniques.name: Magnetometer speaker-leakage eavesdropping (magnetic field emanation capture from speaker voice coils)
techniques.mechanism: Smartphone magnetometer captures electromagnetic emanations from nearby speaker voice coils to reconstruct audio content without acoustic coupling
sources: Matyunin et al. 2019 (MagneticSpy: Exploiting Magnetometer in Mobile Devices for Website and Application Fingerprinting, ACM WiSec)
Guri et al. 2020 (MAGNETO: Covert Channel between Air-Gapped Systems and Nearby Smartphones via CPU-Generated Magnetic Fields)
Zhang et al. 2020 (MagAttack: Eavesdropping on Headphone Magnetic Leakage)

techniques.name: Acoustic-to-neural profiling pipeline (consumer earbud escalation from audio to cognitive exploitation)
techniques.mechanism: Multi-stage escalation chain: speaker repurposing → ear canal fingerprinting → in-ear EEG capture → ML-based cognitive profiling, all from a single compromised consumer earbud
sources: Chain synthesis from: QIF-T0072 → QIF-T0073 → QIF-T0074 → QIF-T0079
Kaveh et al. 2020 (In-ear EEG, IEEE Trans Biomed Eng)
Martinovic et al. 2012 (BCI side channels, USENIX Security)
NEC Corporation 2016 (Ear acoustic authentication)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Integrated hearing health + cognitive monitoring earbuds

techniques.name: Multi-modal biometric fusion attack (cross-sensor identity correlation for persistent tracking)
techniques.mechanism: Fusion of biometric signatures from multiple consumer sensors (acoustic, IMU, RF, optical) to create robust multi-modal identity profile resistant to individual channel disruption
sources: Chain synthesis from: QIF-T0079 (ear canal) + QIF-T0088 (gait) + QIF-T0091 (BLE) + QIF-T0093 (PPG)
Ross et al. 2006 (Handbook of Multibiometrics, Springer)
Hadid et al. 2015 (Biometrics Systems Under Spoofing Attack: An Evaluation Methodology and Lessons Learned, IEEE Signal Processing Magazine)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Multi-modal patient identification for medication safety

techniques.name: Cross-device physiological correlation (phone + watch + earbuds comprehensive health profiling)
techniques.mechanism: Cross-device physiological data correlation across phone + watch + earbuds to build comprehensive health profile exceeding single-device capability
sources: Chain synthesis from: T0075 (ultrasonic) + T0084 (rPPG) + T0089 (tremor) + T0090 (WiFi CSI) + T0093 (PPG)
Majumder et al. 2017 (Wearable Sensors for Remote Health Monitoring, Sensors)
Dunn et al. 2021 (Wearables and the medical revolution, Personalized Medicine)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Multi-device remote patient monitoring for chronic disease management

techniques.name: WiFi + camera passive surveillance fusion (through-wall presence detection with visual identification)
techniques.mechanism: Fusion of WiFi CSI through-wall sensing with camera-based visual identification for persistent surveillance that works through physical barriers
sources: Chain synthesis from: QIF-T0084 (rPPG) + QIF-T0090 (WiFi CSI)
Li et al. 2019 (Wi-Fi See It All: Generative Adversarial Network Augmented Transparent Sensing, ACM SenSys)
Zhao et al. 2018 (Through-Wall Human Pose Estimation Using Radio Signals, CVPR)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Ambient assisted living for elderly monitoring

techniques.name: Consumer-sensor-to-BCI kill chain escalation (pre-implant reconnaissance and cognitive priming via consumer devices)
techniques.mechanism: Full S-domain-to-BCI escalation: consumer sensor reconnaissance builds behavioral/physiological/cognitive baseline that informs and optimizes subsequent BCI attack parameters
sources: Chain synthesis from: S-domain techniques → core TARA BCI techniques
Yuste et al. 2017 (Four ethical priorities for neurotechnologies and AI, Nature)
Ienca & Andorno 2017 (Towards new human rights in the age of neuroscience, Life Sciences Society and Policy)
Landau et al. 2020 (Mind Reading: An Idea Whose Time Has Come?)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Pre-surgical neurological baseline assessment for BCI implant calibration

techniques.name: Neural steganographic encoding (inaudible audio watermarking for covert neural command/biofingerprint channel)
techniques.mechanism: Inaudible audio carriers (ultrasonic 18-22 kHz or infrasonic <20 Hz) or psychoacoustically masked signals embedded within audible content, propagating through consumer audio hardware to auditory nerve
sources: Deshotels 2014 (Inaudible sound as a covert channel)
Carrara 2015 (Ultrasonic data transmission)
Fletcher 1940 (Psychoacoustic masking curves)
Galambos 1981 (40 Hz auditory steady-state response)
Persinger 2003 (Infrasonic effects on vestibular system)
Chaieb et al. 2015 (Neuropsychologia, binaural beat effects)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Auditory brainstem response (ABR) audiometry, ASSR-based hearing threshold estimation, tinnitus masking therapy

techniques.name: Multi-modal keystroke inference via acoustic-optical-RF fusion (password/input recovery without mic/camera permissions)
techniques.mechanism: Temporal fusion of acoustic keystroke emanations, screen optical luminance changes, and WiFi CSI phase variance to reconstruct typed input including passwords
sources: Compagno et al. 2017 (Don't Skype & Type: Acoustic Eavesdropping in VoIP)
Ali et al. 2015 (WiKey: Keystroke Recognition Using WiFi, MobiCom)
Spreitzer et al. 2018 (Systematic classification of side-channel attacks on mobile devices, ACM Computing Surveys)
Zhou et al. 2018 (WiFi CSI human activity recognition survey)
Giancardo et al. 2016 (Keystroke dynamics for Parkinson's, Scientific Reports)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Sensor fusion for motor disorder assessment and digital biomarker collection

techniques.name: Passive facial geometry estimation via display-as-illuminator inverse photometry (presence/orientation detection without camera)
techniques.mechanism: Display photon emission reflects off user's face; ambient light sensor captures aggregate reflected irradiance; inverse photometry estimates facial presence, orientation, and physiological signals
sources: Yang et al. 2016 (Screen light PPG for heart rate)
Davis et al. 2014 (Visual Microphone, SIGGRAPH)
de Haan & Jeanne 2013 (Robust pulse rate from chrominance-based rPPG)
Spreitzer et al. 2018 (Ambient light sensor side channels, ACM Computing Surveys)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Photoplethysmography (PPG) via screen light for contactless vital sign monitoring
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Normal display operation; passive sensing; informed consent for any biometric or health data derivation

techniques.name: SSVEP Frequency Hijack via Imperceptible Display Flicker
techniques.mechanism: Display renders imperceptible flicker patterns at frequencies matching SSVEP response bands. Visual cortex phase-locks to stimulus below conscious awareness. BCI decoder interprets evoked response as
sources: Ming et al. 2023 (J Neural Engineering, 60Hz imperceptible SSVEP, 52.8 bits/min)
Bian et al. 2022 (J Neural Engineering, SSVEP square wave attacks)
Upadhayay & Behzadan 2023 (IEEE SMC, sensory-channel BCI attacks)
Meng et al. 2024 (Future Gen Comp Sys, adversarial EEG filtering)
techniques.therapeutic_analog: High-frequency SSVEP-based BCI for locked-in patients

techniques.name: Neural spoofing
techniques.mechanism: Forging neural identity signatures to impersonate a legitimate BCI node or user, analogous to IP/ARP spoofing in network security
sources: Lopez Bernal et al. 2023 (CACM, Eight Reasons to Prioritize BCI Cybersecurity)

techniques.name: Neural sybil
techniques.mechanism: Creating multiple fake BCI node identities to subvert reputation or consensus mechanisms in distributed neural networks
sources: Lopez Bernal et al. 2023 (CACM, Eight Reasons to Prioritize BCI Cybersecurity)

techniques.name: Neural sinkhole
techniques.mechanism: Attracting and absorbing BCI network traffic by advertising false optimal routing, creating a data collection point analogous to network sinkhole attacks
sources: Lopez Bernal et al. 2023 (CACM, Eight Reasons to Prioritize BCI Cybersecurity)

techniques.name: Neural nonce replay
techniques.mechanism: Capturing and replaying BCI session nonces or authentication tokens to hijack or duplicate neural data sessions
sources: Lopez Bernal et al. 2023 (CACM, Eight Reasons to Prioritize BCI Cybersecurity)

techniques.name: Neuromorphic mimicry attack (synaptic weight tampering)
techniques.mechanism: Tampering with synaptic weight parameters in neuromorphic computing hardware to alter neural network inference without modifying the training data or model architecture
sources: arXiv:2505.17094 (2025, Neuromorphic Mimicry Attacks)
arXiv:2601.16589 (2026, Emerging Threats in Neuromorphic Systems)

techniques.name: Data alignment exploitation
techniques.mechanism: Exploiting misalignment between neural data recording parameters and processing pipeline expectations to introduce systematic bias or silent data corruption
sources: Derived from ABAT (Wu et al. 2024, IEEE TNSRE) and A3E (arXiv:2412.11390, 2024) defense papers

techniques.name: Vestibular balance profiling
techniques.mechanism: Passive recording and analysis of vestibular-evoked responses (VEMPs, caloric responses) to profile an individual's balance system characteristics and susceptibility
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Vestibular evoked myogenic potentials (VEMP) testing

techniques.name: GVS motion illusion injection
techniques.mechanism: Galvanic vestibular stimulation (GVS) delivering subthreshold or suprathreshold current to mastoid processes to induce illusory motion perception or postural sway
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Galvanic vestibular stimulation (GVS) therapy

techniques.name: Vestibular-ocular reflex manipulation
techniques.mechanism: Disrupting the vestibular-ocular reflex (VOR) arc via targeted vestibular stimulation to cause nystagmus, oscillopsia, or gaze instability
techniques.therapeutic_analog: VOR rehabilitation / caloric testing

techniques.name: Cochlear-vestibular crosstalk exploitation
techniques.mechanism: Exploiting the shared innervation of cochlear and vestibular branches of CN VIII to induce vestibular side-effects through auditory stimulation (Tullio phenomenon) or vice versa
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Bone-conducted vibration vestibular rehabilitation

techniques.name: Vestibular overload (vertigo induction)
techniques.mechanism: Overwhelming the vestibular system via sustained high-intensity stimulation to cause acute vertigo, nausea, spatial disorientation, and postural collapse
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Canalith repositioning (Epley maneuver) / vestibular habituation therapy
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Overload stimulation exceeds therapeutic parameters — defensive detection only; therapeutic equivalent uses controlled sub-threshold exposure

techniques.name: Cumulative tissue excitability shift
techniques.mechanism: Chronic subthreshold stimulation causing gradual shift in neural tissue excitability thresholds through long-term potentiation or depression of synaptic efficacy
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) long-term protocols

techniques.name: Progressive reward pathway desensitization
techniques.mechanism: Systematic reduction of dopaminergic reward circuit sensitivity through repeated supraphysiological stimulation, causing anhedonia and motivational deficit
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Deep brain stimulation of nucleus accumbens / ventral capsule

techniques.name: Incremental memory consolidation interference
techniques.mechanism: Targeted disruption of hippocampal sharp-wave ripple complexes during sleep to prevent memory consolidation without affecting other sleep functions
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Targeted memory reactivation (TMR) / sleep-dependent memory consolidation research

techniques.name: Cumulative speech degradation
techniques.mechanism: Progressive degradation of speech motor control through chronic low-level disruption of Broca's area or corticobulbar pathways, causing cumulative articulatory imprecision
techniques.therapeutic_analog: rTMS of Broca's area for post-stroke aphasia rehabilitation

techniques.name: Delayed emotional baseline shift
techniques.mechanism: Gradual modulation of amygdala-prefrontal connectivity to shift emotional baseline toward persistent dysthymia or anxiety without acute detectable events
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Neurofeedback training for emotional regulation

techniques.name: Latent cognitive decline via chronic electrode micromotion
techniques.mechanism: Chronic micromotion of implanted electrodes causing progressive gliosis, impedance drift, and degradation of neural recording/stimulation fidelity, leading to gradual cognitive decline
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Chronic DBS electrode maintenance / impedance management

techniques.name: Sleep architecture manipulation
techniques.mechanism: Targeted alteration of sleep stage architecture via closed-loop stimulation during specific sleep phases to disrupt restorative sleep without preventing sleep onset
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Closed-loop auditory stimulation for slow-wave sleep enhancement

techniques.name: Chronic epileptogenic focus creation (kindling)
techniques.mechanism: Repeated low-intensity electrical stimulation (kindling paradigm) creating a self-sustaining epileptogenic focus that persists after stimulation cessation
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Kindling model in epilepsy research (preclinical)
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Kindling is a harm model — no therapeutic application. Detection and prevention only. Any deliberate kindling in humans would constitute criminal assault

techniques.name: Structural motor pathway reorganization
techniques.mechanism: Chronic manipulation of motor cortex stimulation parameters causing maladaptive cortical reorganization of motor maps, leading to persistent motor control changes
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Motor cortex rTMS / tDCS for stroke rehabilitation

techniques.name: Autobiographical narrative coherence disruption
techniques.mechanism: Disrupting hippocampal-prefrontal circuits responsible for autobiographical memory retrieval and self-narrative coherence, causing fragmented sense of personal history
techniques.therapeutic_analog: ECT (electroconvulsive therapy) / TMS for treatment-resistant depression
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Identity disruption requires highest-tier consent; psychiatric evaluation before and after; narrative coherence assessment mandatory

techniques.name: Longitudinal neural fingerprint extraction
techniques.mechanism: Longitudinal collection of stable neural response patterns (ERP latencies, spectral fingerprints, connectivity signatures) to build a persistent biometric identifier that survives session boundaries
techniques.therapeutic_analog: ERP/EEG biomarker tracking for treatment response monitoring

techniques.name: Progressive visual response mapping
techniques.mechanism: Systematic mapping of visual evoked potentials and steady-state visual evoked potentials (SSVEPs) across stimulus parameters to profile visual cortex response characteristics
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Visual evoked potential (VEP) diagnostics

techniques.name: Acute fear circuit activation
techniques.mechanism: Direct stimulation of amygdala fear circuits to induce acute fear, panic, or threat-perception states without external environmental threat
techniques.therapeutic_analog: DBS of amygdala / extinction learning facilitation
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Fear circuit activation is a harm vector outside controlled research; detection and prevention only; anxiolytic rescue protocol required

techniques.name: Hedonic tone suppression
techniques.mechanism: Suppression of ventral striatum and orbitofrontal cortex reward-related activity to reduce hedonic tone, causing loss of pleasure in normally enjoyable activities
techniques.therapeutic_analog: DBS of subcallosal cingulate / ventral capsule for depression

techniques.name: Emotional flooding (affect overload)
techniques.mechanism: Simultaneous overstimulation of multiple limbic structures (amygdala, insula, anterior cingulate) to overwhelm emotional regulation capacity, causing uncontrollable affect surges
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Emotional processing therapy / exposure therapy (controlled activation)
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Emotional flooding exceeds any therapeutic parameter — detection and prevention only; therapeutic analog uses controlled graded exposure

techniques.name: Empathy circuit disruption
techniques.mechanism: Disruption of mirror neuron system and anterior insula circuits responsible for empathic processing, causing diminished affective empathy while potentially preserving cognitive empathy
techniques.therapeutic_analog: TMS of temporoparietal junction for social cognition research

techniques.name: Speech production hijacking
techniques.mechanism: Injection of motor commands into speech production pathways via stimulation of motor cortex mouth/larynx areas, causing involuntary vocalizations or speech
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Speech neuroprosthesis / brain-to-speech BCI

techniques.name: Comprehension interference
techniques.mechanism: Disruption of Wernicke's area and auditory association cortex processing to impair language comprehension while preserving speech production and hearing
techniques.therapeutic_analog: rTMS of Wernicke's area for tinnitus / auditory hallucinations

techniques.name: Visual cortex overstimulation
techniques.mechanism: Excessive photic or electrical stimulation of primary visual cortex (V1) to induce phosphenes, visual distortion, cortical spreading depression, or photosensitive seizures
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Visual cortex prosthesis (Orion / Second Sight) / phosphene mapping
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Overstimulation exceeds therapeutic parameters — detection and prevention only; therapeutic visual prostheses use carefully mapped individual thresholds

techniques.name: Cochlear implant overstimulation
techniques.mechanism: Delivery of excessive electrical stimulation through cochlear implant electrodes to cause pain, acoustic shock, tinnitus, or vestibular side-effects via current spread to adjacent structures
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Cochlear implant therapy
mitigations: Safety ceiling: Overstimulation exceeds device comfort levels — detection and prevention only; cochlear implants use individual threshold/comfort mapping

techniques.name: Neural pathway chaining (off-target propagation exploitation)
techniques.mechanism: Exploiting neural pathway interconnectedness to cause stimulation effects that propagate beyond the intended target region, producing off-target cognitive, motor, or emotional effects through synaptic
techniques.therapeutic_analog: Connectome-informed DBS targeting / network-level stimulation
